factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a significant headwind across multiple sectors, particularly impacting the Power and Oil & Gas markets. Challenges include soft demand due to energy efficiency, renewable energy penetration, geopolitical factors, and macroeconomic instability. Additionally, there are risks related to liquidity stress at GE Capital and potential higher liabilities from unresolved legal matters.

evidence.text: The power market continued to be soft during the first half of 2018 due to energy efficiency, renewable energy penetration, and delays in expected orders. In addition, excess capacity in developed markets, continued pressure in oil and gas applications, and macroeconomic and geopolitical environments have created softening demand for gas turbines.

GE Capital - A smaller GE Capital continues to have exposure to credit, insurance and other risks and, in the event of future adverse developments, may not be able to meet its business and financial objectives without taking further actions at GE Capital or additional capital contributions by GE.
